name: Júcar Canyons and Jalance Castle Loop
name_original: El Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes — Vuelta gravel desde Cofrentes
description: This difficult 27.3-mile gravel touring cycling route in El Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes offers challenging terrain and scenic views.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cofrentes offers routes through a landscape shaped by the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, featuring dramatic canyons and varied elevation. The region includes an extinct volcano, Cerro de Agras, and is bordered by mountain ranges such as Boqueron and Caroig. Pine forests and Mediterranean vegetation cover much of the terrain, providing diverse natural settings for cycling. Reservoirs like Embarcaderos and Cortes are also prominent features. Best touring cycling routes around Cofrentes The most popular touring cycling route is Júcar Canyons and Jalance Castle Loop, a 27.3 miles (43.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and views of the Júcar Canyons. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cabriel River – Cofrentes Water Wheel loop from Cofrentes, a difficult 18.9 miles (30.5 km) path. This route follows the Cabriel River, offering scenic views of the river and surrounding natural features. Local touring cyclists also love the View of the Forest – Views of the Júcar River loop from Castilblanques, a 53.5 miles (86.1 km) trail leading through forested areas and alongside the Júcar River, often completed in about 6 hours 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Cofrentes is defined by river canyons, volcanic terrain, and mountainous vistas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Cofrentes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 130 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cofrentes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes can I explore around Cofrentes?
A: Cofrentes offers a diverse network of over 30 touring cycling routes, catering to various preferences. These routes navigate through the region's unique landscapes, including river canyons, volcanic terrain, and mountainous vistas.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level for touring cycling routes in Cofrentes?
A: The majority of touring cycling routes in Cofrentes are considered challenging, with 25 out of 30 routes rated as difficult. These often feature significant elevation gains, providing rewarding rides with spectacular views. There are also 5 moderate routes available for those seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Cofrentes?
A: Yes, many routes in Cofrentes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917631875/jucar-canyons-and-jalance-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar Canyons and Jalance Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, offering views of the dramatic Júcar Canyons.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: Cofrentes's touring cycling routes feature a mix of terrain. While many segments are paved, you will also encounter gravel and unpaved sections, offering a varied cycling experience. The landscape is characterized by river canyons, volcanic hills, and forested mountain ranges.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions to see while cycling in Cofrentes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle alongside the dramatic Júcar Canyons, explore areas near the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, or visit the extinct Cerro de Agras volcano. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4376712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embarcaderos Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the picturesque El Corbinet Waterfall are also popular spots.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles accessible by bike?
A: Absolutely. The impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4578187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched atop the Cerro de Agras volcano, is a prominent landmark. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle – Puerto de Casas del Río loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt; can bring you close to this historical site. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5570151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jalance Castle&lt;/a&gt; along some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917631875/jucar-canyons-and-jalance-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar Canyons and Jalance Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about Cofrentes?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cofrentes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning scenery, the challenging yet rewarding elevation gains, and the unique blend of river canyons and volcanic landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Cabriel River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31521507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabriel River – Cofrentes Water Wheel loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult route that closely follows the Cabriel River, offering scenic views of the river and its surrounding natural features.

Q: Can I find routes that lead through forested areas?
A: Certainly. The region is covered with Mediterranean vegetation, including extensive pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40638622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pocico Spring – View of the Forest loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for cycling through these natural woodlands.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Cofrentes?
A: Cofrentes offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling, allowing you to fully enjoy the region's diverse landscapes without extreme heat.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the dramatic landscapes of Cofrentes offer numerous opportunities for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4376696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pileta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1772531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt; are just a couple of examples where you can pause and take in the stunning vistas of the Júcar River and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Júcar River?
A: Many routes in Cofrentes provide excellent views of the Júcar River, especially where it carves through canyons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40638877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Forest – Views of the Júcar River loop from Castilblanques&lt;/a&gt; is a longer route that specifically highlights the river's beauty as it winds through the landscape.
tips: The castle of Andalusian origin, dates from the 12th century with a very interesting history. It is accessed by a concrete path, which allows you to appreciate very nice views of the town of Jalance, the Cofrentes-Ayora Valley and the Cofrentes Nuclear Power Plant.
Perfect place for a stop with benches, there is water but not for drinking.

This bridge marks the point where the Cabriel riverbed joins the Júcar riverbed. And together they continue downstream towards the Cortes millstone. A very picturesque place with a beautiful landscape a few meters from the town of Cofrentes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the El Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes — Gravel Tour?
faqs.answer: This challenging gravel tour is approximately 43.9 km (27.3 miles) long. Based on its difficulty and terrain, it typically takes around 3 hours and 7 minutes to complete, though this can vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this gravel tour?
faqs.answer: The El Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes — Gravel Tour is categorized as difficult. It's considered a 'Hard bike ride' requiring 'very good fitness'. You should be prepared for significant elevation gain and potentially challenging unpaved sections where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this gravel tour?
faqs.answer: As a gravel tour, you should expect a mix of surfaces. While some parts might be paved, significant portions will be unpaved roads, tracks, and possibly rougher sections. The area is mountainous, so prepare for climbs and descents on varied terrain, including pine forests and hills covered with rosemary and thyme.

faqs.question: Where does the El Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes — Gravel Tour start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The tour starts from Cofrentes. While specific parking details for the exact trailhead aren't provided, Cofrentes is a village with general parking options. It's advisable to check local parking availability upon arrival, especially near the town center or common starting points for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: What are the scenic highlights and landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers incredible landscapes and historical sites. You'll pass through pine forests, hills, and charming villages. Notable highlights include the impressive Jalance Castle, the tranquil Fuente del Tobarro Recreation Area, and the unique Confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers. The region is also known for the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park, offering spectacular gorge views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this gravel tour?
faqs.answer: The region experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are generally the best times to visit, offering milder temperatures and pleasant conditions for cycling. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, though often still suitable for cycling on sunny days.

faqs.question: Does this gravel tour connect with other trails in the region?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with Los Cañones del Júcar, La Ruta de Jalance, El Campichuelo, Jarafuel, Muela de Juey y el Castillico, Las Aldeas de Basta y Alcance, La Chirrichana, Los Castillos del Valle, Camino del Balneario, El Alto de Alcola, Alto de Alcola, and Senda los Burros.

faqs.question: Is the El Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes — Gravel Tour dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact gravel tour aren't detailed, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in natural areas in Spain, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local signage or official park guidelines for the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes region before heading out with your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this tour?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this gravel tour in the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes region. However, if you plan to enter specific natural parks or protected areas along the route, it's always wise to check for any local regulations or restrictions that might apply.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near charming villages such as Cofrentes, Ayora, Jalance, Jarafuel, and Teresa de Cofrentes. These villages offer opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation, allowing you to refuel and rest during or after your ride. The region is well-suited for active tourism.

faqs.question: What should I know about the challenging sections of this gravel tour?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and 'very good fitness' requirement, be prepared for segments with significant elevation gain and unpaved, potentially rough surfaces. Some sections might be steep enough to require you to dismount and push your bike. Ensure your gravel bike is well-maintained and suitable for varied, demanding terrain.

waypoints.name: Confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers
